title: Instrument cleanup tracer to log weird volume removal flake
body:
Debug for #23913, I though if we have no idea which process is nuking the volume then we need to figure this out. As there is no reproducer we can (ab)use the cleanup tracer. Simply trace all unlink syscalls to see which process deletes our special named volume. Given the volume name is used as path on the fs and is deleted on volume rm we should know exactly which process deleted it the next time hopefully.

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@edsantiago @mheon PTAL. Hopefully this will be enough to find the root cause the next time we get this flake.

Seems to work: https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5875612148039680/cleanup_tracer/podman-cleanup-tracer.log
```
Special issue 23913 volume deleted by pid 240123: /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/bin/podman volume rm v-t396-a9sahjtw-ebpf-debug-23913 
Special issue 23913 volume deleted by pid 240123: /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/bin/podman volume rm v-t396-a9sahjtw-ebpf-debug-23913 
```
Of course this rm command is expected as it is the normal teardown. But it will be interesting to see what we find when the flake happens
